macro_segments.title: Distinguishing Ritual Atonement from Personal Transformation: Kapara vs. Taharah

micro_segments.title: Rabbi's Position: Yom Kippur Atones Without Teshuva, But Kapara Differs from Taharah

macro_segments.title: Determining Genuine Teshuva: Resisting Repeated Temptation in Identical Circumstances

Identical Circumstances Requirement: Woman, Time, and Place Must Be the Same

macro_segments.title: Public vs. Private Confession: Reconciling Two Biblical Verses on Covering Sin

First Resolution: Public Sins Require Public Confession; Private Sins Remain Private

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yosei b'Rabbi Yehuda: Forgiveness for First Three Sins; Fourth Time Denied
Rambam's Interpretation: Divine Discounting Rather Than Denial of Teshuva

If You Backslide: Return to Viduy; If You Persist in Agonizing: You Are Like a Dog Returning to Vomit

macro_segments.title: Dispute Over Repeated Confession: Rabbi Eliezer ben Yaakov vs. Apparent First Opinion

macro_segments.title: Public Leaders as Models: Moshe's Transparency vs. David's Discretion Regarding Sin

Balance of Concerns: Preserving Human Dignity vs. Public Accountability

micro_segments.title: Tosefta Ruling: Publicize Hypocrites to Prevent Desecration of God's Name
Biblical Support: If a Righteous One Does Wrong, God Will Cause Him to Stumble
Rashi's Explanation: Preventing the Misled From Emulating the Hypocrite

micro_segments.title: Teshuva of Even Hardened Sinners Prevents Punishment, Even if Decreed

Authority and Leadership: Positions Bury Their Holders; Judges Risk Punishment

macro_segments.title: Good and Evil in This World vs. The Next: Righteous Punished Now, Wicked Deferred

micro_segments.title: The Righteous Receive Punishment Now; Their Merit Accrues to Future Generations

Case of Achav: His Humbling Extends His Years, But Disaster Comes to His Sons
Case of Moshe: His Punishment in This World Spares Future Generations

micro_segments.title: Examples: Aharon's Sons Spared; Kna'an's Descendants Burdened by His Debt
